metric_value: World leader in end-to-end multimedia network solutions in the Integrated Enterprise
notes: Aberdeen accepts Nortel's self-characterization as world leader. Reflects 1997 market position prior to dot-com bubble peak.

metric_value: Magellan ATM/Frame Relay switches #1 in multimedia networking
notes: Nortel's market position claim for Magellan ATM switches.

metric_value: 2,000 professionals (80% engineers) in 100 countries
notes: Global Enterprise Services division headcount at time of study.

metric_value: Data traffic surpassed voice traffic on world's networks for first time in 1996
notes: Significant milestone cited to support multimedia networking investment thesis.

metric_value: $435,000 per year in reduced line costs for 1,600-user 10-county frame relay network
notes: Customer testimonial. Nortel Magellan Passport enabled multimedia network. Payback projected within 24-36 months.

metric_value: 24-36 months payback for Nortel Magellan multimedia network
notes: Customer stated: 'I couldn't afford to not build this network.'

metric_value: Nortel well-positioned as market leader; best-of-breed benchmark for networking industry
notes: Aberdeen concludes Nortel is the benchmark against which all other networking providers should be measured.

metric_value: Nortel filed for bankruptcy January 14, 2009; liquidated by end of 2009
notes: Assets sold piecemeal 2009-2011. Wireless to Ericsson ($1.13B), enterprise to Avaya ($915M), optical/Ethernet to Ciena ($774M), patents to Apple-led consortium ($4.5B). Peak market cap had been $297-400B.

metric_value: Accounting fraud discovered 2003-2004; executives fired and charged; $6B+ revenue misreported 1998-2003
notes: CEO Frank Dunn fired for financial mismanagement. Charges ultimately dropped in 2013 but damage to investor trust was irreparable.

metric_value: $297-400 billion peak market cap; ~35-38% of Toronto Stock Exchange
notes: At peak in 2000, Nortel was the 9th most valuable company in the world by some measures.

metric_value: Approximately 60,000-70,000 employees at time of study (pre-peak)
notes: Peak was 95,000; at bankruptcy ~30,000. 1997 figure estimated based on growth trajectory.

metric_value: ATM will consolidate multimedia (voice/data/video) traffic on enterprise and carrier backbones
notes: Core thesis: consolidated ATM multimedia backbones replacing legacy TDM and X.25 networks.

metric_value: IP/Ethernet/MPLS displaced ATM for most enterprise and carrier applications by mid-2000s
notes: ATM's complexity and cost made it lose out to simpler IP-based solutions. Nortel's heavy ATM dependence was a strategic liability.

metric_value: Six sigma (99.999%) reliability required for multimedia calls and carrier networks
notes: Nortel's telco heritage in voice reliability cited as key differentiator for multimedia networks.

metric_value: Nortel Magellan Passport enables true multimedia network carrying voice, data, and video for telemedicine
notes: Customer testimonial for a 10-county telemedicine network.

metric_value: Cisco lacks integrated LAN/WAN ATM solutions and end-to-end integration capability
notes: Aberdeen/Nortel position Cisco as inferior in end-to-end solutions. Proved incorrect: Cisco dominated enterprise networking.

metric_value: Cisco became the dominant enterprise networking vendor; Nortel collapsed
notes: Cisco's IP-centric strategy proved correct. Nortel's ATM focus and financial mismanagement led to bankruptcy.

metric_value: Many large enterprises facing make vs. buy decision on WAN management
notes: Aberdeen cites globalization and network complexity as drivers of WAN outsourcing decisions.

metric_value: Network services becoming carrier/WAN-centric; NSP market emerging
notes: Aberdeen predicts growth of Network Service Provider market. VPN and managed WAN services did grow substantially.

metric_value: Computer Telephony Integration as Nortel competitive advantage vs. routing-only vendors
notes: Nortel ahead of Cisco and IBM in CTI, circuit switching, and messaging at time of study.

metric_value: Nortel-Sun Microsystems partnership for next-generation multimedia client-server telephones
notes: Goal: enable multimedia network access via next-gen client-server telephones. Sun later acquired by Oracle in 2010.
